2026-04-22T05:15:52.8044846Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-22T05:15:52.8045249Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-22T05:15:52.8045596Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m

Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"parent":{"description":"Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.","type":"string"}},"required":["title","body"],"type":"object"},"name":"create_issue"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). 2026-04-22T05:18:51.9725925Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-22T05:18:39.932512524Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## Documentation Gap\n\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights`\n**Service directory:** `sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/`\n**Triggered by:** PR #58443 (commit e2671314d) by @ArthurMa1978\n\n## What Changed\n\nPR #58443 promoted `Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights` to its **first stable release (1.0.0)** as part of a batch ARM SDK refresh. Version `1.0.0` was stamped on 2026-04-21.\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- **Key concepts section is empty/generic** — only links to the generic Azure SDK introduction page. It should describe CustomerInsights-specific concepts: `HubResource`, Profiles, Interactions, Connectors, Relationships, Views, KPIs, and Authorization Policies.\n- **No inline code examples** — the \"Examples\" section only points to the generic `aka.ms/azuresdk-net-mgmt-samples` link without any service-specific snippets. At minimum, creating a Hub and listing its profiles should be demonstrated.\n- **No Samples test files** — the `tests/` directory has no `Samples/` subfolder with snippet-annotated NUnit test methods. These are required to generate README snippets via `Update-Snippets.ps1`.\n- **README header and \"Getting Started\" still use beta-era generic boilerplate** — for a GA release the description should be updated to drop references to \"Public Preview\" and use definitive language about capabilities.\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\n\n### Step 1: Modify files\n\n#### Edit `sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/README.md`\n\nReplace the `## Key concepts` section (currently a single generic link) with a service-specific section:\n\n```markdown\n## Key concepts\n\n### Hub\n\nA **Hub** (`HubResource`) is the top-level Customer Insights resource that lives in an Azure Resource Group. All other Customer Insights resources (Profiles, Interactions, Connectors, etc.) are scoped under a Hub.\n\n### Profiles and Interactions\n\n**Profiles** represent the customer entities (e.g., a person or account). **Interactions** represent customer activities (e.g., a purchase or a support call). Both are defined as schema definitions inside a Hub.\n\n### Connectors and Connector Mappings\n\n**Connectors** define the data source (e.g., Azure Blob Storage) used to ingest data. **Connector Mappings** describe how incoming data maps to Profiles or Interactions.\n\n### Views, KPIs, and Relationships\n\nThese sub-resources let you surface aggregated metrics, define data relationships across Profiles, and create custom views over the data.\n```\n\nReplace the `## Examples` section with a placeholder that will be filled in by the snippet tool:\n\n```markdown\n## Examples\n\n### Create a Hub\n\n```C# Snippet:CreateHub\n```\n\n### List Hubs in a subscription\n\n```C# Snippet:ListHubs\n```\n```\n\n#### Create `sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/tests/Samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs`\n\nCreate the file with the following content:\n\n```csharp\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\n\nusing System;\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\nusing Azure.Identity;\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights;\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights.Models;\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\nusing NUnit.Framework;\n\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights.Tests.Samples\n{\n    public class Sample1_HelloWorld : SamplesBase\u003cCustomerInsightsManagementTestEnvironment\u003e\n    {\n        [Test]\n        [SyncOnly]\n        [Ignore(\"Only verifying that the sample compiles\")]\n        public async Task CreateHub()\n        {\n            #region Snippet:CreateHub\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroups().GetAsync(\"\u003cresource-group-name\u003e\");\n\n            HubCollection hubs = resourceGroup.GetHubs();\n            HubData hubData = new HubData(AzureLocation.WestUS);\n            ArmOperation\u003cHubResource\u003e lro = await hubs.CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \"\u003chub-name\u003e\", hubData);\n            HubResource hub = lro.Value;\n            Console.WriteLine($\"Hub created: {hub.Data.Name}\");\n            #endregion\n        }\n\n        [Test]\n        [SyncOnly]\n        [Ignore(\"Only verifying that the sample compiles\")]\n        public async Task ListHubs()\n        {\n            #region Snippet:ListHubs\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroups().GetAsync(\"\u003cresource-group-name\u003e\");\n\n            await foreach (HubResource hub in resourceGroup.GetHubs())\n            {\n                Console.WriteLine($\"Hub: {hub.Data.Name}, Location: {hub.Data.Location}\");\n            }\n            #endregion\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\n\nThe snippets are defined in the test file above using `#region Snippet:\u003cSnippetName\u003e` / `#endregion`.\n\n**Snippet 1: `CreateHub`**\n- Test file: `sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/tests/Samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs` — method `CreateHub`\n- README location: under the `### Create a Hub` heading in `## Examples`\n- Placeholder: `` ```C# Snippet:CreateHub `` followed by closing ` ``` `\n\n**Snippet 2: `ListHubs`**\n- Test file: `sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/tests/Samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs` — method `ListHubs`\n- README location: under the `### List Hubs in a subscription` heading in `## Examples`\n- Placeholder: `` ```C# Snippet:ListHubs `` followed by closing ` ``` `\n\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\n\nThe README must contain these sections in this order. Current status:\n\n| Section | Status |\n|---------|--------|\n| Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Present |\n| Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Present |\n| Getting started → Authenticate the client | ✅ Present |\n| Key concepts | ⚠️ Too generic — needs CustomerInsights-specific content (see Step 1) |\n| Examples | ❌ Missing inline code — only a generic link (see Step 1 + Step 2) |\n| Troubleshooting | ✅ Present |\n| Next steps | ✅ Present |\n| Contributing | ✅ Present |\n\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone.\n\n### Step 4: Validate\n\nRun these commands in order.
